component_identification.content: Viticulture Essential Edition includes main board, six vineyard mats, worker and grande worker meeples, temporary worker, structure tokens, grape and wine tokens, lira coins, wake-up roosters, VP and residual markers, first player token, and multiple card decks: vine, wine order, summer visitor, winter visitor, plus mama and papa setup cards. Players manage fields, crush pad, cellar tracks, and structures on personal mats.

core_game_loop.content: Each year has spring, summer, fall, winter, and year end. In spring players choose wake-up position for turn order and bonus. Summer uses workers for planting, structures, and card actions. Fall grants visitor cards. Winter handles harvest, winemaking, training, and filling orders. Year end ages grapes and wines and recalls workers.

detailed_rules.content: Worker placement is seasonal and space-limited. Grande worker can use an already-occupied action once per year, providing tactical protection against blocking. Vine cards determine legal planting and must respect field value limits. Harvest converts field vines into grape values on crush pad tracks. Winter actions convert grapes to wine if cellar level supports that wine type. Wines and grapes age at year end up to track caps. Wine order cards provide points and residual income and are the primary scoring engine. Structures unlock vital capabilities such as cellar upgrades, yoke, and vine requirements. Visitor cards are powerful but timing-locked by season deck color. The wake-up chart creates an order versus bonus tradeoff every year. Common mistakes include producing wine beyond cellar limits, forgetting season restrictions, and racing to points without reliable residual income or order flow.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Game-end trigger is a player reaching twenty points, but the current year is completed before final winner is declared. Highest point total wins; ties break by most lira, then total wine value in cellar, then grape value on crush pad. Players may exceed twenty points.

beginner_strategy.content: Build cellar and planting infrastructure before overdrawing cards. Create steady residual income early for long-term tempo. Use grande worker as a safety tool for critical blocked actions. Choose wake-up positions based on your immediate bottleneck. Fill orders on a repeatable cadence rather than one burst and stall.

metadata.notes: Rewrite sprint with rulebook-text as primary source.
